host.displayName: The Vortex Project — Pine Hollow Research Station
host.description: A solarpunk citizen-science research station in Sevierville, TN. Three-domain observational science: ground vegetation/acoustics (Solaris), sky/atmosphere/space (ObserverBot), and cross-domain archive (Archivator).

entries.description: Vegetation analysis via Earth Engine (NDVI, biomass, canopy, terrain, hydrology), bird detection summaries from BirdNET-Pi, phenology tracking, and homestead infrastructure monitoring. Can answer weather-related questions in context of vegetation and land change.
representativeQueries: what was the NDVI trend at Pine Hollow this spring
show me bird detections from last night
when did the sugar maple leaf out this year
how has the pond recovery progressed since 2016

entries.description: Monitors and reports on the full atmospheric column: solar activity, space weather, aurora, air quality, ISS passes, meteor showers, astronomy events. Uses web search and public APIs for current conditions.
representativeQueries: what's the air quality like over Pine Hollow
when's the next ISS pass visible from Sevierville
is the aurora forecast active tonight

entries.description: Cross-references observations across ground and sky domains. Correlates bird detections with vegetation change, insect activity with temperature, migration timing with canopy green-up. Manages the searchable research thread archive.
representativeQueries: were there bird detections during last week's solar flare
show me research threads about pond recovery
what observations correlate with temperature changes this season
